dataset: SecRepo auth.log and Loghub SSH logs

pi_items.source: Task description instructs saving the report as 'SSH_Brute_Force_Sessions', which export_result.sh normalizes to 'ssh_brute_force_sessions'. Confirmed by verifier.py logic.
Task description instructs creating a dashboard named 'SSH_Session_Analysis', which export_result.sh normalizes to 'ssh_session_analysis'. Confirmed by verifier.py logic.
install_splunk.sh and setup_splunk.sh confirm real-world logs from SecRepo and Loghub are ingested into the 'security_logs' index.
Task description instructs using the 'transaction' command. verifier.py explicitly checks for the presence of this command in the SPL query.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world SSH authentication data downloaded from SecRepo and Loghub (Zenodo record 8196385), which is ingested into the 'security_logs' index. The agent is explicitly required to use the 'transaction' SPL command to group events. The resulting query must be saved as a report named 'SSH_Brute_Force_Sessions' (normalized to 'ssh_brute_force_sessions') and a dashboard named 'SSH_Session_Analysis' (normalized to 'ssh_session_analysis') containing at least two visualization panels.
